text:
Yuhirai (ユヒライ, Yuhirai) is a member of Prince Halkenburg's Private Army. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 362 

Appearance

Yuhirai has a lean build, short dark hair, and narrow eyes. As a Royal Bodyguard, he wears a black suit and a tie. 

Personality

Yuhirai, like the rest of the bodyguards under Prince Halkenburg's service, seems to share the same will as their employer. He appears to be less emotional than Shedule. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 372 

Plot

Succession Contest arc

On the first day of the voyage, 9 th Prince Halkenburg retires from the departure ceremony back to Room 1009 where he finds all his guards lying on the floor unconscious before collapsing himself right after. He awakens several hours later in Room 1009's master bedroom and barges out, only to see his bodyguards all unpacking things and organizing his room as if nothing had happened, and wonders if what he saw was a dream. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 366 

Later that night, Halkenburg is contacted by Room 1014 and agrees to send his bodyguards to learn Nen under Kurapika. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 368 Shedule and Yuhirai are sent to learn Nen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 369 

At 9 a.m. the next morning, Shedule and Yuhirai assemble at Room 1014 for Kurapika's Nen lessons. Shedule looks at the mark of a pinion on the back of his left hand and whispers his guess that it was the work of Halkenburg's Nen beast to Yuhirai, who agrees and says that it is the mark of resolve that Halkenburg always espoused. 

Yuhirai later witnesses the death of Barrigen at the hands of four snake-like beasts. Yuhirai surmises that the snake attack was a ploy to disrupt the Nen training and Shedule believes that it was an attack by a Prince who didn't send a representative. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 370 Following the murder, Kurapika tells his students to decide whether to stay or leave. Yuhirai and Shedule discuss it over and decide to remain, so Kurapika teaches them his first exercise. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 371 Kurapika takes notice of his and Shedule's aura output and enquires if they are testing him. They defend themselves and, for safety and secrecy reasons, decide Yuhirai is appointed to speak with Kurapika after the lecture. 

Sakata insists on participating, and Yuhirai agrees to it. He tells Kurapika about the marks that have appeared on the back of Halkenburg's and his bodyguards' hands. Kurapika determines he has only half-awakened to Nen and convenes it is due to Halkenburg's Nen beast. Yuhirai resolves to keep sharing information despite Sakata's presence, as forging an alliance with Prince Woble will also benefit Halkenburg. 

On the third day, Halkenburg has his personal guards gather for a moment of silence for 12 th Prince Momoze after her death the night before; Yuhirai stands between Shedule and Kontasutin. Unbeknownst to them, their unison generates an enormous amount of aura observed by Shikaku, guard of 1 st Prince Benjamin, watching over them. Shikaku watches Halkenburg's Guardian Spirit Beast perched on his shoulders and reports the matter to Benjamin. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 375 

At 10:05 a.m. on the third day of the voyage, 4 th Prince Tserriednich's guard Myuhan is killed during Nen lessons in Room 1014, and his corpse is found in a similar state to Barrigen's. Maor, Captain of 5 th Prince Tubeppa's Guard and sent by her to attend Nen lessons, and Satobi, Captain of 2 nd Queen Duazul's Guard, assigned to guard 7 th Prince Luzurus and sent by him to attend Nen lessons, accuse Kurapika of luring the men of enemies of Woble under the promise of learning Nen to eliminate them one by one. Belerainte, Hunter and guard of 13 th Prince Marayam defends Kurapika and blames Myuhan for acting carelessly and using the bathroom while in hostile territory. Kurapika calls Belerainte over to thank him privately, though the act is watched with suspicion by the other participants of the class, including Yuhirai. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 376 

On the seventh day of the voyage, Yuhirai is still taking part in Kurapika's Nen lessons. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 382 Like everyone else, he begins to have doubts about the training, since they always repeat the same exercise. The following day, he observes Kurapika's Water Divination. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 386 Yuhirai waits in the hall until the end of the session while Ladiolus, Maor, Yuri, and Satobi are called one by one to the main bedroom supposedly to perform Water Divination. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 388 

On the tenth day, Yuhirai turns up at Room 1014 for Kurapika's Nen lesson. Kurapika and Bill invite Longhi into Room 1014's master bedroom to have her Nen divined, but takes an unnaturally long amount of time, prompting Sakata to knock and ask if anything is wrong. Kurapika steps out and says that negotiations are taking a while because they and Longhi are unable to agree on the terms of the truce, but promises to clear up any doubts the participants have later. Kurapika steps back in, and a while later returns to Room 1014's living room together with Longhi. Kurapika apologizes and explains that they are still unable to agree on the details, and invites Tenftory in to have his Nen divined. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 401 

Later that day, Tenftory reports to 3 rd Prince Zhang Lei that all of the students who have taken Kurapika's Nen training are able to utilize Nen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 389 

Around noon on the twelfth day of the voyage, twelve of Halkenburg's guards, including Yuhirai, are seen outside the general hospital as Halkenburg's pallbearers, to carry his casket from Tier 3 to Tier 1. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 406 Yuhirai and Halkenburg's other guards carry Halkenburg's casket up the staircase from Tier 3 to Tier 2. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 407 

Abilities & Powers

Nen

Due to the power of Prince Halkenburg's Guardian Spirit Beast, Yuhirai has reached a state of half-awakening in which he is unable to see aura, but he has a superior output compared to someone who only just opened their aura nodes. However, Kurapika speculated that by receiving the Nen beast's mark Yuhirai was made to share its Limitations and penalties, which might be as severe as the loss of one's life if the conditions of the ability are breached. 

Manga Appearances

Trivia

For the purposes of Kurapika's Nen training, Yuhirai is a "prayer".
